response.justification: Excellent software engineer with strong fundamentals in testing, architecture, and cross-platform development, but fundamentally misaligned with this C++ industrial automation role. C++ experience is minimal and dated (6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015); recent 11 years focused on TypeScript, JavaScript, Node.js, and D. Critically lacks experience in industrial automation, IoT, secure development lifecycle standards, industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA), and the multi-threaded, message-broker systems core to the position. While capable of learning, the role demands mid-senior C++ productivity immediately.
strengths: Demonstrated mastery of software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, clean architecture, and proven mentoring across organizations
Strong architectural and systems-design thinking with distributed systems, networking, and infrastructure experience
Proven leadership and communication ability; history of technical mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ expertise is minimal and outdated; last professional C++ work was 11 years ago; primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js
No experience in industrial automation domain, IoT development, or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, SDL, secure development practices)
No background in multi-threaded systems, message brokers, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA), or networked embedded systems
